subject: Chemistry
solution:
OXO PROCESS (Hydroformylation) is  the addition of a hydrogen atom and a formyl group to the molecule of a compound containing a double bond by reaction with hydrogen and carbon monoxide, the chief product being one or more aldehydes.

CH3-CH2-CH=CH2→Rh catalystH2/COCH3CH2CH2CHO

subject: Chemistry
solution:
CARBYL amine given by 1° amine

Carbylamine reaction can be used to identify primary amino group in a given organic compound. When primary amines are treated with chloroform and alcoholic KOH, they give isocyanides (carbylamines) having very unpleasant smell which can be easily detected.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
α -ANOMER OF MALTOSE

maltose is disaccharides of α -D- glucopyranose by C1-C4 glycosidic linkage

Maltose or malt sugar is a disaccharide composed of two glucose molecules connected with an alpha (1,4) glycosidic bond.

Maltose is a reducing sugar. It's two glucose molecules must be linked in such a way as to leave one anomeric carbon that can open to form an aldehyde group.

solution:
Complex
(i) FeF63-Fe3+⇒[Ar]3 d5, F- is WFL

n=5,  μ=35 B.M

(ii)CoNH363+⇒Co3+Ar3 d6,NH3 is SFL

n=0, μ=0 B.M

(iii) NiCl42-Ni2+⇒Ar3 d8,Cl-→WFL

n=2, μ=8 B.M

(iv) CuNH342+Cu2+⇒Ar3 d9,NH3→SFL

n=1, μ=3 B.M

Thus correct order of spin only magnetic moment is

(i)>(iii)>(iv)>(ii)

solution:
Both statements are correct. Normally rain water has pH of 5.6 due to the presence of H+ ions formed by the reaction of rain water with carbon dioxide present in the atmosphere.

Normal rain has a pH of about 5.6. This is because it reacts with carbon dioxide in the atmosphere and forms mildly acidic carbonic acid before it becomes rain.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
For testing of halogens, Nitric acid is added to the sodium extract because if CN- or S2- are present then they will be oxidised and removed before the test of halides.

NaCN+HNO3→NaNO3+HCN↑
Na2S+HNO3→NaNO3+H2S↑
Nilnic acid decomposed NaCN & Na2S, else they precipitate in test & misquite the resolve

subject: Chemistry

Presence of electron withdrawing groups such as nitro group enhance the acidic strength of phenol and the presence of electron releasing groups such as alkyl group decreases the acidic strength of phenol.

 

2>3>4>1

subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
solution:
Zone refining is used for the purification of indium.

Zone refining is based on the principle that the impurities are more soluble in the melt than in the solid state of the metal. 

This method is very useful for producing semiconductor and other metals of very high purity, e.g., germanium, silicon, boron, gallium.

Nitration of aniline in strong acidic medium also gives m-nitroaniline because in acidic (strong) medium aniline is present as anilinium ion.

Benzene is an aromatic compound which shows the characteristic reaction of electrophilic aromatic substitution reactions. This is because the reaction is initiated by an electrophile.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Carbon monoxide does not react with water to form an acid.

The carbon monoxide molecule is one that won't react with water at all. As the acidic oxide has a higher oxidation state, it reacts with water to produce acid, and reacts with base to produce salt. 

CO2+H2O→H2CO3

C+H2Osteam→CO+H2

CH4+H2O→CO+3H2C3H8+H2O→3CO+H2 both reactions are carried out at 1270 K temp. with Ni catalyst

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Correct order of bond dissociation enthalpy of halogens is Cl2>Br2>F2>I2.

Due to inter electronic repulsions F-F bond becomes weak and easily broken.

Bond dissociation energy of halogen family decreases down the group as the size of atom increases.

solution:
Shortest distance between curves is always along common normal.

Let Px0, y0 be any point on the parabola x2=2y.

Clearly, slope of line x-y=1 is 1. 

Hence, slope of perpendicular line is -1.

dydxP=Slope of tangent at P 

Therefore, -1dydxP=Slope of normal=-1

⇒dydxP=1

⇒x0=1 ∴y0=12

⇒P1,12

∴ Shortest distance =1-12-112+12=122

.=10p±1

∴ Number of odd two digit numbers =45

⇒4cosx+y2·cosx-y2-4cos2x+y2=1=sin2x-y2+cos2x-y2

⇒4cos2x+y2+cos2x-y2-4cosx+y2·cosx-y2+sin2x-y2=0

⇒2cosx+y2-cosx-y22+sin2x-y2=0

⇒sinx-y2=0 and cosx+y2=12cosx-y2

⇒x=y and cosx=12=cosy

∴ sinx=32

⇒sinx+cosy=1+32

options: Statement <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi></math> is false but Statement <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>II</mi></math> is true.
Statement <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi></math> is true but Statement <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>II</mi></math> is false.
Both Statement <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi></math> and Statement <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>II</mi></math> are true.
Both Statement <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi></math> and Statement <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>II</mi></math> are false.
solution:
The translational kinetic energy & rotational kinetic energy both obey Maxwell's distribution
independent of each other.
Translational kinetic energy of diatomic molecules =32kT
Rotational kinetic energy of diatomic molecules =22kT
So statement I is true but statement II is false.
